visa_types.name: Studies (more than 90 days)
visa_types.documents_required:
Documents Checklist (English) – National Student Visa (https://www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch/de) 
Documents in Thai language must be translated into English or into one of the Swiss national languages by a translation office.
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Click here
visa_types.visa_fees: Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (https://www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch/de).
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This is intended for those who are going to study in Switzerland for a duration of more than 90 days.
You must submit your visa application directly at the Swiss Regional consular center Bangkok. Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This is intended for those who are going to study in Switzerland for a duration of more than 90 days.
You must submit your visa application directly at the Swiss Regional consular center Bangkok. Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch).

## Visa Fees
Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (https://www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch/de).

## Documents Required
Documents Checklist (English) – National Student Visa (https://www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch/de) 
Documents in Thai language must be translated into English or into one of the Swiss national languages by a translation office.
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Click here

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Times
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Business visa is required if during any portion of the visit, the applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. e.g., meetings, conferences, trainings, trade fairs,
                                                                        etc.
You must book an appointment to submit your visa application and have your biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on your chosen appointment date and time. Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/che/book-an-appointment) to book your appointment.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Business visa is required if during any portion of the visit, the applicant intends to conduct business or commercial activities. e.g., meetings, conferences, trainings, trade fairs,
                                                                        etc.
You must book an appointment to submit your visa application and have your biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on your chosen appointment date and time. Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/che/book-an-appointment) to book your appointment.

## Visa Fees effective from the 15th June 2025

[Table:  Business]
The visa fee of 35 EUR is stipulated by a visa facilitation agreement with the EU for citizens of: Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, North Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia, Ukraine, Moldova, Georgia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cabo Verde

- The following applicants are exempted from visa fee:
- Family member of Swiss citizen
- Diplomatic and official passport holders
- School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training - TO BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE
- Researchers travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research - TO BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE
- Representatives of non-profit organisations, aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events by non-profit organisations – TO
                                                                            BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE
 Below is the service charge when applying for at VFS Switzerland VAC: 

[Table:  Tourist]
- The applicable visa fees in THB depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
- Applicants who are exempted from the visa fees need to pay only the service fee
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

## Documents Required
Documents Checklist (English) – Schengen Business Visa 
Dokumentencheckliste (Deutsch) – Schengenvisum für geschäftliche Zwecke 
 Liste de vérification des documents (Français) – Visa Schengen pour affaires 
Documents in Thai language must be translated into English or into one of the Swiss national languages by a translation office.
Please refer to the following document in order to obtain further information on required documents and options to have these completed directly at the visa application centre.
Documents Checklist (English) – COMPLETING DOCUMENTS AT THE CENTRE 
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/che/travel-insurance)

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Travel Medical Insurance
 A medical insurance is compulsory for visitors to Switzerland and other Schengen states. You may provide either a policy from an insurance company based in Europe or in Thailand. Please
                                                                        note that only certain insurance companies in Thailand are accepted. The insurance must cover at least 30 000 Euro or 1 500 000 Baht and include costs for medical treatment, hospitalization
                                                                        and repatriation due to injury or medical reasons. The insurance must be valid in all Schengen states and cover the total length of the visit.
Please note, the Insurance policy will be considered valid, only if it has the Insurance Company stamp and authorized signature.
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/xruTdPDsGJOVRJf2oIUue/00014ff617c4dc6033de6ef963e9a8e7/list-of-travel-medical-insurance-curent-updated.pdf) to download the list of approved insurance companies in Thailand.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

visa_types.name: Visa Types
visa_types.overview:
Schengen visa
Citizens of some non-EU countries are required to hold a valid Schengen visa when travelling to the Schengen area, which is a group of European countries that allows a person, who’s been
                                                                admitted to the Schengen Area, to travel without any further form of border control within the Schengen Area.
Current Schengen States are: Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, the Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Romania, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
A Schengen visa is a short stay visa, i.e., for stays up to 90 days, allowing its holder to circulate in the Schengen Area.
A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Mission of the Schengen State, which will be the main destination (i.e., where the most nights will be spent) during a single trip to the Schengen
                                                                Area.
A single trip is considered when an applicant enters the Schengen Area, stays there for a certain period and eventually leaves the Schengen Area.
If an equal amount of nights will be spent in different Schengen States during a single trip to the Schengen Area, then the Schengen visa must be obtained with the Mission of the Schengen State,
                                                                which will be entered first.
The documentation related to the means of travel and accommodation must cover the complete itinerary of the single trip to the Schengen Area; i.e. printouts of intended flight bookings or other
                                                                proof of intended ways of travel, not only for the travel from and to the Schengen Area, but also for the travel between the different Schengen States, when more than 1 Schengen State will
                                                                be visited during the single trip.
Each Schengen visa application must fully comply with the documents required for the respective visa category and can only be submitted up to six months (180 days) before the intended departure
                                                                date.
The acceptance of a Schengen visa application and supporting documents by VFS Global does not guarantee the issuance of a Schengen visa.
The issuance of a Schengen visa, the Schengen visa validity period, the number of entries and amount of travel days are at the sole discretion of the Mission.
If a visa application is returned by the Mission requesting missing documents or any other missing/ incorrect information, the applicant must accept responsibility for any delay that may occur
                                                                in the visa application processing time.
Holders of valid Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control upon arrival in the Schengen Area and are not necessarily guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries, even though
                                                                they may hold a valid Schengen visa for these countries.
Following visa categories are considered short-stay visas (Schengen visas):
- Schengen Tourist  Visa
- Schengen Business  Visa
- Schengen  Visitor Visa
- Schengen Visa for spouse and registered partner of Swiss or EU/EEA citizens
- Schengen Visa for foreign children of the spouse of a Swiss or EU/EEA citizen
- Schengen  Student Visa
National visa
For a Swiss long-stay visa, you are required to submit your application directly at the Regional Consular Centre of the Swiss Embassy in Bangkok.
Please click here (https://www.eda.admin.ch/bangkok) for the link to the website of the Swiss Embassy in Thailand.
Following visa categories are considered long-stay visas (National visas):
- National  Student Visa
- National Visa for family reunion (after marriage in Thailand)
- National Visa for marriage in Switzerland

- National  Student Visa
- National Visa for family reunion (after marriage in Thailand)
- National Visa for marriage in Switzerland

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicants traveling to Switzerland to visit family or friends including girlfriend/boyfriend may apply for this visa.
A Declaration of Sponsorship may be required by the Regional consular center Bangkok, at their discretion.
You must book an appointment to submit your visa application and have your biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on your chosen appointment date and time. Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/che/book-an-appointment) to book your appointment.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicants traveling to Switzerland to visit family or friends including girlfriend/boyfriend may apply for this visa.
A Declaration of Sponsorship may be required by the Regional consular center Bangkok, at their discretion.
You must book an appointment to submit your visa application and have your biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on your chosen appointment date and time. Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/che/book-an-appointment) to book your appointment.

- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes

                                                                        and repatriation due to injury or medical reasons. The insurance must be valid in all Schengen states and cover the total length of the visit.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This visa is solely for the purpose of leisure travel including vacationing, cruising, sightseeing and other recreational activities. This visa is given for a travel period maximum of
                                                                        180 days.
You must book an appointment to submit your visa application and have your biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on your chosen appointment date and time. Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/che/book-an-appointment) to book your appointment.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The visa for family reunification with a Swiss or an EU citizen is given in request for a national visa in order to join husband/wife who is a national of Switzerland or the European Union and who lives in Switzerland.
You must submit your visa application directly at the Swiss Regional consular center Bangkok. Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch).
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The visa for family reunification with a Swiss or an EU citizen is given in request for a national visa in order to join husband/wife who is a national of Switzerland or the European Union and who lives in Switzerland.
You must submit your visa application directly at the Swiss Regional consular center Bangkok. Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch).

## Visa Fees
Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (https://www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch/de).

## Documents Required
Documents Checklist (English) – National Visa for family reunion after marriage in Thailand (https://www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch/de) 
Documents in Thai language must be translated into English or into one of the Swiss national languages by a translation office.
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Click here

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
STCC members that have registered for the Business Fast Track (BFT) Program and intend to conduct business in Switzerland can apply for this category.
BFT (Business Fast Track) members can benefit from a faster and leaner visa application process to Switzerland. Participants of this program need less documents for the visa application and can enjoy a facilitated submission at VFS Global by either using Walk-In without appointment into our Premium Lounge (THB2200), use the BFT appointment category in our booking portal or availing our Visa At Your Doorstep service.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
STCC members that have registered for the Business Fast Track (BFT) Program and intend to conduct business in Switzerland can apply for this category.
BFT (Business Fast Track) members can benefit from a faster and leaner visa application process to Switzerland. Participants of this program need less documents for the visa application and can enjoy a facilitated submission at VFS Global by either using Walk-In without appointment into our Premium Lounge (THB2200), use the BFT appointment category in our booking portal or availing our Visa At Your Doorstep service.

## Visa Fees effective from the 15th June 2025

[Table:  Business Fast Track - BFT (for registered members only)]
The visa fee of 35 EUR is stipulated by a visa facilitation agreement with the EU for citizens of: Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, North Macedonia, Montenegro, Serbia, Ukraine, Moldova, Georgia, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Cabo Verde

- The following applicants are exempted from visa fee:
- Family member of Swiss citizen
- Diplomatic and official passport holders
- School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training - TO BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE
- Researchers travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research - TO BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE
- Representatives of non-profit organisations, aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events by non-profit organisations – TO
                                                                            BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE
 Below is the service charge when applying for at VFS Switzerland VAC: 

[Table:  Tourist]
- The applicable visa fees in THB depend on the current exchange rate, which can be changed without any notifications
- Applicants who are exempted from the visa fees need to pay only the service fee
- The fees are non-refundable and non-transferable.

## Documents Required
Documents Checklist (English) – Business Fast Track Visa (BFT) 
Documents in Thai language must be translated into English or into one of the Swiss national languages by a translation office.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Travel Medical Insurance
 A medical insurance is compulsory for visitors to Switzerland and other Schengen states. You may provide either a policy from an insurance company based in Europe or in Thailand. Please
                                                                        note that only certain insurance companies in Thailand are accepted. The insurance must cover at least 30 000 Euro or 1 500 000 Baht and include costs for medical treatment, hospitalization
                                                                        and repatriation due to injury or medical reasons. The insurance must be valid in all Schengen states and cover the total length of the visit.
Please note, the Insurance policy will be considered valid, only if it has the Insurance Company stamp and authorized signature.
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/xruTdPDsGJOVRJf2oIUue/00014ff617c4dc6033de6ef963e9a8e7/list-of-travel-medical-insurance-curent-updated.pdf) to download the list of approved insurance companies in Thailand.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

visa_types.name: National Visa
visa_types.overview:
A national visa is intended for a person wishing to enter Switzerland for a stay of more than 180 days.
It is subject to the authorization of the cantonal migration authority competent for the intended place of residence.
The following types are falling in the category of a national visa: long-term student, family reunification, marriage in Switzerland, etc. National visas are applied for directly at the Regional Consular Center of the Embassy of Switzerland in Thailand. Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch).
visa_types.full_text:

Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This is intended for those travelling to Switzerland in order to study within a short course or to attend training courses, e.g., language courses, summer camps, etc.
You must book an appointment to submit your visa application and have your biometric data registered at VFS Visa Application Center on your chosen appointment date and time. Please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/tha/en/che/book-an-appointment) to book your appointment.
visa_types.full_text:

This is intended for those travelling to Switzerland in order to study within a short course or to attend training courses, e.g., language courses, summer camps, etc.

Please note, the Insurance policy will be considered valid, only if it has the Insurance Company stamp and authorized signature.
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/xruTdPDsGJOVRJf2oIUue/00014ff617c4dc6033de6ef963e9a8e7/list-of-travel-medical-insurance-curent-updated.pdf) to download the list of approved insurance companies in Thailand.

Overview
- Family reunion for child
This is intended for children of Swiss citizens or children of the spouse/partner of a Swiss citizen who will stay longer than 180 days in Switzerland.
- Family reunion for partner of a Swiss, EU or EEA citizen
This is intended for partners (not married, not registered partnership) who will stay in Switzerland longer than 180 days. 
You must submit your visa application directly at the Swiss Regional consular center Bangkok. Kindly revert to the information of the Swiss Regional Consular Centre in Bangkok: www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch (https://www.schweiz-thailand.eda.admin.ch/de).

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa
visa_types.overview:
(For a maximum stay of 90 days per 180-day period)
If you wish to visit Switzerland for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Switzerland.
A short-stay Schengen visa allows the holder to transit through or remain in Swiss territory and all other Schengen States for a maximum period of three months (90 days) within a period of 180
                                                                days from the entry date into the Schengen Area.
Short-stay Schengen visa  applications cannot be submitted more than 180 days in advance before the  intended travel date. 
However, they must be submitted at least 15 calendar days prior to the proposed date of travel. VFS Global is the outsourcing office responsible for the reception and release of passports for
                                                                short-stay Schengen visa applications.
visa_types.full_text:
(For a maximum stay of 90 days per 180-day period)
If you wish to visit Switzerland for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Switzerland.
A short-stay Schengen visa allows the holder to transit through or remain in Swiss territory and all other Schengen States for a maximum period of three months (90 days) within a period of 180
                                                                days from the entry date into the Schengen Area.
Short-stay Schengen visa  applications cannot be submitted more than 180 days in advance before the  intended travel date. 
However, they must be submitted at least 15 calendar days prior to the proposed date of travel. VFS Global is the outsourcing office responsible for the reception and release of passports for
                                                                short-stay Schengen visa applications.

This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This is intended for an applicant who is travelling to Switzerland as the child of a spouse of a Swiss or EU/EEA citizen. This visa is given for a travel period of maximum of 180 days.
If you fall under this category, you must submit your application directly at the Swiss Regional consular center Bangkok. You can find the appointment booking here: https://swissembassybkk.online-booking.me/#/en/home (https://swissembassybkk.online-booking.me/#/en/home)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This is intended for an applicant who is travelling to Switzerland as the child of a spouse of a Swiss or EU/EEA citizen. This visa is given for a travel period of maximum of 180 days.
If you fall under this category, you must submit your application directly at the Swiss Regional consular center Bangkok. You can find the appointment booking here: https://swissembassybkk.online-booking.me/#/en/home (https://swissembassybkk.online-booking.me/#/en/home)

## Visa Fees

## Visa Fees effective from the 15th June 2025

[Table:  Foreign children of the spouse of a Swiss or EU/EEA citizen]
- The following applicants are exempted from visa fees:
- Family member of Swiss citizen
- Diplomatic and official passport holders
- School pupils, students, post graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training - TO BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE
- Researchers travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research - TO BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE
- Representatives of non-profit organisations, aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events by non-profit organisations –
                                                                                TO BE EVALUATED CASE BY CASE

## Documents Required
Documents Checklist (English) – Schengen Visa for foreign children of the spouse of a Swiss or EU/EEA citizen 
Dokumentencheckliste (Deutsch) – Schengenvisum für ausländische Kinder von Ehegatten von Schweizer oder EU/EWR Bürgern Bürgern Bürgern 
Liste de documents (Français) – Visa Schengen pour enfants étrangers d’une conjointe d’un citoyen suisse ou d’un citoyen l'UE/EEE 
Documents in Thai language must be translated into English or into one of the Swiss national languages by a translation office.
Travel medical insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, Click here

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Travel Medical Insurance
A medical insurance is not required this category. However, you may purchase it for your travel.

## Processing Time
Usually, processing time for visa applications is 15 working days (starting from the time when the Embassy or Consulate received the application, not including the transportation time), counting from when all requested documents have been submitted. This period may be extended in specific cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is required, for example, when an applicant must come to an interview or submit additional documentation requested by the Embassy or Consulate. During certain times of the year, the processing time can be longer due to the high volume of applications. It is recommended to submit your visa application at least two weeks prior to date of travel date and if possible, even earlier ahead of the holiday period. 
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
